Related actions:

* [[JoinAction]]: Unlike JoinAction, RegisterAction implies you are registering to be a user of a service, *not* a group/team of people.
* [[FollowAction]]: Unlike FollowAction, RegisterAction doesn't imply that the agent is expecting to poll for updates from the object.
* [[SubscribeAction]]: Unlike SubscribeAction, RegisterAction doesn't imply that the agent is expecting updates from the object.
label: RegisterAction

comments:
The act of rejecting to/adopting an object.

Related actions:

* [[AcceptAction]]: The antonym of RejectAction.
label: RejectAction

comments: The act of giving money in return for temporary use, but not ownership, of an object such as a vehicle or property. For example, an agent rents a property from a landlord in exchange for a periodic payment.
label: RentAction

comments: A reservation for a rental car.\n\nNote: This type is for information about actual reservations, e.g. in confirmation emails or HTML pages with individual confirmations of reservations.
label: RentalCarReservation

comments: A structured value representing repayment.
label: RepaymentSpecification

comments: The act of editing a recipient by replacing an old object with a new object.
label: ReplaceAction

comments:
The act of responding to a question/message asked/sent by the object. Related to [[AskAction]].

Related actions:

* [[AskAction]]: Appears generally as an origin of a ReplyAction.
label: ReplyAction

comments: A Report generated by governmental or non-governmental organization.
label: Report

comments:
The [[ReportageNewsArticle]] type is a subtype of [[NewsArticle]] representing
 news articles which are the result of journalistic news reporting conventions.

In practice many news publishers produce a wide variety of article types, many of which might be considered a [[NewsArticle]] but not a [[ReportageNewsArticle]]. For example, opinion pieces, reviews, analysis, sponsored or satirical articles, or articles that combine several of these elements.

The [[ReportageNewsArticle]] type is based on a stricter ideal for "news" as a work of journalism, with articles based on factual information either observed or verified by the author, or reported and verified from knowledgeable sources.  This often includes perspectives from multiple viewpoints on a particular issue (distinguishing news reports from public relations or propaganda).  News reports in the [[ReportageNewsArticle]] sense de-emphasize the opinion of the author, with commentary and value judgements typically expressed elsewhere.

A [[ReportageNewsArticle]] which goes deeper into analysis can also be marked with an additional type of [[AnalysisNewsArticle]].
label: ReportageNewsArticle

comments: A patient-reported or observed dosing schedule for a drug or supplement.
label: ReportedDoseSchedule

comments: A Research Organization (e.g. scientific institute, research company).
label: ResearchOrganization

comments: A Research project.
label: ResearchProject

comments: Researchers.
label: Researcher

comments: Describes a reservation for travel, dining or an event. Some reservations require tickets. \n\nNote: This type is for information about actual reservations, e.g. in confirmation emails or HTML pages with individual confirmations of reservations. For offers of tickets, restaurant reservations, flights, or rental cars, use [[Offer]].
label: Reservation

comments: A group of multiple reservations with common values for all sub-reservations.
label: ReservationPackage

comments: Enumerated status values for Reservation.
label: ReservationStatusType

comments:
Reserving a concrete object.

Related actions:

* [[ScheduleAction]]: Unlike ScheduleAction, ReserveAction reserves concrete objects (e.g. a table, a hotel) towards a time slot / spatial allocation.
label: ReserveAction

comments: A reservoir of water, typically an artificially created lake, like the Lake Kariba reservoir.
label: Reservoir

comments: The action of resetting the password of a device or application.
label: ResetPasswordAction

comments: The place where a person lives.
label: Residence

comments:
A resort is a place used for relaxation or recreation, attracting visitors for holidays or vacations. Resorts are places, towns or sometimes commercial establishments operated by a single company (source: Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia, see http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Resort).

See also the dedicated document on the use of schema.org for marking up hotels and other forms of accommodations.
label: Resort

comments: The therapy that is concerned with the maintenance or improvement of respiratory function (as in patients with pulmonary disease).
label: RespiratoryTherapy

comments: A restaurant.
label: Restaurant

comments: A diet restricted to certain foods or preparations for cultural, religious, health or lifestyle reasons.
label: RestrictedDiet

comments: The act of resuming a device or application which was formerly paused (e.g. resume music playback or resume a timer).
label: ResumeAction

comments: The act of returning to the origin that which was previously received (concrete objects) or taken (ownership).
label: ReturnAction

comments: Enumerates several kinds of policies for product return fees.
label: ReturnFeesEnumeration

comments: Enumerates several types of return labels for product returns.
label: ReturnLabelSourceEnumeration

comments: Enumerates several types of product return methods.
label: ReturnMethodEnumeration

comments: A review of an item - for example, of a restaurant, movie, or store.
label: Review

comments: The act of producing a balanced opinion about the object for an audience. An agent reviews an object with participants resulting in a review.
label: ReviewAction

comments: A [[NewsArticle]] and [[CriticReview]] providing a professional critic's assessment of a service, product, performance, or artistic or literary work.
label: ReviewNewsArticle

comments: A river (for example, the broad majestic Shannon).
label: RiverBodyOfWater

comments: Represents additional information about a relationship or property. For example a Role can be used to say that a 'member' role linking some SportsTeam to a player occurred during a particular time period. Or that a Person's 'actor' role in a Movie was for some particular characterName. Such properties can be attached to a Role entity, which is then associated with the main entities using ordinary properties like 'member' or 'actor'.\n\nSee also [blog post](https://blog.schema.org/2014/06/16/introducing-role/).
label: Role

comments: A roofing contractor.
label: RoofingContractor

comments:
A room is a distinguishable space within a structure, usually separated from other spaces by interior walls (source: Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia, see http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Room).

See also the dedicated document on the use of schema.org for marking up hotels and other forms of accommodations.
label: Room

comments: The act of notifying an event organizer as to whether you expect to attend the event.
label: RsvpAction

comments: RsvpResponseType is an enumeration type whose instances represent responding to an RSVP request.
label: RsvpResponseType

comments: Specialized software environment that provides the essential infrastructure, libraries, and services required to execute a program.
label: RuntimePlatform

comments: Event type: Sales event.
label: SaleEvent

comments: An [[Article]] whose content is primarily [[satirical]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Satire) in nature, i.e. unlikely to be literally true. A satirical article is sometimes but not necessarily also a [[NewsArticle]]. [[ScholarlyArticle]]s are also sometimes satirized.
label: SatiricalArticle

comments:
A schedule defines a repeating time period used to describe a regularly occurring [[Event]]. At a minimum a schedule will specify [[repeatFrequency]] which describes the interval between occurrences of the event. Additional information can be provided to specify the schedule more precisely.
      This includes identifying the day(s) of the week or month when the recurring event will take place, in addition to its start and end time. Schedules may also
      have start and end dates to indicate when they are active, e.g. to define a limited calendar of events.
label: Schedule

comments:
Scheduling future actions, events, or tasks.

Related actions:

* [[ReserveAction]]: Unlike ReserveAction, ScheduleAction allocates future actions (e.g. an event, a task, etc) towards a time slot / spatial allocation.
label: ScheduleAction

comments: A scholarly article.
label: ScholarlyArticle

comments: A school.
label: School

comments: A School District is an administrative area for the administration of schools.
label: SchoolDistrict

comments: A screening of a movie or other video.
label: ScreeningEvent

comments: A piece of sculpture.
label: Sculpture

comments: A sea (for example, the Caspian sea).
label: SeaBodyOfWater

comments:
The act of searching for an object.

Related actions:

* [[FindAction]]: SearchAction generally leads to a FindAction, but not necessarily.
label: SearchAction

comments: A Search and Rescue organization of some kind.
label: SearchRescueOrganization

comments: Web page type: Search results page.
label: SearchResultsPage

comments: A media season, e.g. TV, radio, video game etc.
label: Season

comments: Used to describe a seat, such as a reserved seat in an event reservation.
label: Seat

comments: This is the [[Action]] of navigating to a specific [[startOffset]] timestamp within a [[VideoObject]], typically represented with a URL template structure.
label: SeekToAction

comments: A self-storage facility.
label: SelfStorage

comments: The act of taking money from a buyer in exchange for goods or services rendered. An agent sells an object, product, or service to a buyer for a price. Reciprocal of BuyAction.
label: SellAction

comments:
The act of physically/electronically dispatching an object for transfer from an origin to a destination. Related actions:

* [[ReceiveAction]]: The reciprocal of SendAction.
* [[GiveAction]]: Unlike GiveAction, SendAction does not imply the transfer of ownership (e.g. I can send you my laptop, but I'm not necessarily giving it to you).
label: SendAction

comments: An art forms that use images deployed in a specific order for the purpose of graphic storytelling (i.e., narration of graphic stories) or conveying information. Examples of SequentialArt are Franco-Belgian Bande Dessinée, Comics in the USA and 漫画 (Manga) in Japan.
label: SequentialArt

comments: A Series in schema.org is a group of related items, typically but not necessarily of the same kind. See also [[CreativeWorkSeries]], [[EventSeries]].
label: Series

comments: A service provided by an organization, e.g. delivery service, print services, etc.
label: Service

comments: A means for accessing a service, e.g. a government office location, web site, or phone number.
label: ServiceChannel

comments: ServicePeriod represents a duration with some constraints about cutoff time and business days. This is used e.g. in shipping for handling times or transit time.
label: ServicePeriod

comments: The act of distributing content to people for their amusement or edification.
label: ShareAction

comments: Printed music, as opposed to performed or recorded music.
label: SheetMusic

comments: ShippingConditions represent a set of constraints and information about the conditions of shipping a product. Such conditions may apply to only a subset of the products being shipped, depending on aspects of the product like weight, size, price, destination, and others. All the specified conditions must be met for this ShippingConditions to apply.
label: ShippingConditions

comments: ShippingDeliveryTime provides various pieces of information about delivery times for shipping.
label: ShippingDeliveryTime

comments: A ShippingRateSettings represents re-usable pieces of shipping information. It is designed for publication on an URL that may be referenced via the [[shippingSettingsLink]] property of an [[OfferShippingDetails]]. Several occurrences can be published, distinguished and matched (i.e. identified/referenced) by their different values for [[shippingLabel]].
label: ShippingRateSettings

comments: ShippingService represents the criteria used to determine if and how an offer could be shipped to a customer.
label: ShippingService

comments: A shoe store.
label: ShoeStore

comments: A shopping center or mall.
label: ShoppingCenter

comments: Short story or tale. A brief work of literature, usually written in narrative prose.
label: ShortStory

comments: Residence type: Single-family home.
label: SingleFamilyResidence

comments: A navigation element of the page.
label: SiteNavigationElement

comments: Enumerates common size groups for various product categories.
label: SizeGroupEnumeration

comments: Size related properties of a product, typically a size code ([[name]]) and optionally a [[sizeSystem]], [[sizeGroup]], and product measurements ([[hasMeasurement]]). In addition, the intended audience can be defined through [[suggestedAge]], [[suggestedGender]], and suggested body measurements ([[suggestedMeasurement]]).
label: SizeSpecification

comments: Enumerates common size systems for different categories of products, for example "EN-13402" or "UK" for wearables or "Imperial" for screws.
label: SizeSystemEnumeration

comments: A ski resort.
label: SkiResort

comments: Event type: Social event.
label: SocialEvent

comments: A post to a social media platform, including blog posts, tweets, Facebook posts, etc.
label: SocialMediaPosting

comments: A software application.
label: SoftwareApplication

comments: Computer programming source code. Example: Full (compile ready) solutions, code snippet samples, scripts, templates.
label: SoftwareSourceCode

comments: The action that takes in a math expression and directs users to a page potentially capable of solving/simplifying that expression.
label: SolveMathAction

comments: A placeholder for multiple similar products of the same kind.
label: SomeProducts

comments: A SpeakableSpecification indicates (typically via [[xpath]] or [[cssSelector]]) sections of a document that are highlighted as particularly [[speakable]]. Instances of this type are expected to be used primarily as values of the [[speakable]] property.
label: SpeakableSpecification

comments:
A SpecialAnnouncement combines a simple date-stamped textual information update
      with contextualized Web links and other structured data.  It represents an information update made by a
      locally-oriented organization, for example schools, pharmacies, healthcare providers,  community groups, police,
      local government.

For work in progress guidelines on Coronavirus-related markup see [this doc](https://docs.google.com/document/d/14ikaGCKxo50rRM7nvKSlbUpjyIk2WMQd3IkB1lItlrM/edit#).

The motivating scenario for SpecialAnnouncement is the [Coronavirus pandemic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2019%E2%80%9320_coronavirus_pandemic), and the initial vocabulary is oriented to this urgent situation. Schema.org
expect to improve the markup iteratively as it is deployed and as feedback emerges from use. In addition to our
usual [Github entry](https://github.com/schemaorg/schemaorg/issues/2490), feedback comments can also be provided in [this document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1fpdFFxk8s87CWwACs53SGkYv3aafSxz_DTtOQxMrBJQ/edit#).


While this schema is designed to communicate urgent crisis-related information, it is not the same as an emergency warning technology like [CAP](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Common_Alerting_Protocol), although there may be overlaps. The intent is to cover
the kinds of everyday practical information being posted to existing websites during an emergency situation.

Several kinds of information can be provided:

We encourage the provision of "name", "text", "datePosted", "expires" (if appropriate), "category" and
"url" as a simple baseline. It is important to provide a value for "category" where possible, most ideally as a well known
URL from Wikipedia or Wikidata. In the case of the 2019-2020 Coronavirus pandemic, this should be "https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=2019-20\_coronavirus\_pandemic" or "https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q81068910".

For many of the possible properties, values can either be simple links or an inline description, depending on whether a summary is available. For a link, provide just the URL of the appropriate page as the property's value. For an inline description, use a [[WebContent]] type, and provide the url as a property of that, alongside at least a simple "[[text]]" summary of the page. It is
unlikely that a single SpecialAnnouncement will need all of the possible properties simultaneously.

We expect that in many cases the page referenced might contain more specialized structured data, e.g. contact info, [[openingHours]], [[Event]], [[FAQPage]] etc. By linking to those pages from a [[SpecialAnnouncement]] you can help make it clearer that the events are related to the situation (e.g. Coronavirus) indicated by the [[category]] property of the [[SpecialAnnouncement]].

Many [[SpecialAnnouncement]]s will relate to particular regions and to identifiable local organizations. Use [[spatialCoverage]] for the region, and [[announcementLocation]] to indicate specific [[LocalBusiness]]es and [[CivicStructure]]s. If the announcement affects both a particular region and a specific location (for example, a library closure that serves an entire region), use both [[spatialCoverage]] and [[announcementLocation]].

The [[about]] property can be used to indicate entities that are the focus of the announcement. We now recommend using [[about]] only
for representing non-location entities (e.g. a [[Course]] or a [[RadioStation]]). For places, use [[announcementLocation]] and [[spatialCoverage]]. Consumers of this markup should be aware that the initial design encouraged the use of [[about]] for locations too.

The basic content of [[SpecialAnnouncement]] is similar to that of an [RSS](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/RSS) or [Atom](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Atom_(Web_standard)) feed. For publishers without such feeds, basic feed-like information can be shared by posting
[[SpecialAnnouncement]] updates in a page, e.g. using JSON-LD. For sites with Atom/RSS functionality, you can point to a feed
with the [[webFeed]] property. This can be a simple URL, or an inline [[DataFeed]] object, with [[encodingFormat]] providing
media type information, e.g. "application/rss+xml" or "application/atom+xml".
label: SpecialAnnouncement

comments: Any branch of a field in which people typically develop specific expertise, usually after significant study, time, and effort.
label: Specialty

comments: A sporting goods store.
label: SportingGoodsStore

comments: A sports location, such as a playing field.
label: SportsActivityLocation

comments: A sports club.
label: SportsClub

comments: Event type: Sports event.
label: SportsEvent

comments: Represents the collection of all sports organizations, including sports teams, governing bodies, and sports associations.
label: SportsOrganization

comments: Organization: Sports team.
label: SportsTeam

comments: A spreadsheet file.
label: SpreadsheetDigitalDocument

comments: A stadium.
label: StadiumOrArena

comments: A state or province of a country.
label: State

comments: A statement about something, for example a fun or interesting fact. If known, the main entity this statement is about can be indicated using mainEntity. For more formal claims (e.g. in Fact Checking), consider using [[Claim]] instead. Use the [[text]] property to capture the text of the statement.
label: Statement

comments:
A StatisticalPopulation is a set of instances of a certain given type that satisfy some set of constraints. The property [[populationType]] is used to specify the type. Any property that can be used on instances of that type can appear on the statistical population. For example, a [[StatisticalPopulation]] representing all [[Person]]s with a [[homeLocation]] of East Podunk California would be described by applying the appropriate [[homeLocation]] and [[populationType]] properties to a [[StatisticalPopulation]] item that stands for that set of people.
The properties [[numConstraints]] and [[constraintProperty]] are used to specify which of the populations properties are used to specify the population. Note that the sense of "population" used here is the general sense of a statistical
population, and does not imply that the population consists of people. For example, a [[populationType]] of [[Event]] or [[NewsArticle]] could be used. See also [[Observation]], where a [[populationType]] such as [[Person]] or [[Event]] can be indicated directly. In most cases it may be better to use [[StatisticalVariable]] instead of [[StatisticalPopulation]].
label: StatisticalPopulation

comments: [[StatisticalVariable]] represents any type of statistical metric that can be measured at a place and time. The usage pattern for [[StatisticalVariable]] is typically expressed using [[Observation]] with an explicit [[populationType]], which is a type, typically drawn from Schema.org. Each [[StatisticalVariable]] is marked as a [[ConstraintNode]], meaning that some properties (those listed using [[constraintProperty]]) serve in this setting solely to define the statistical variable rather than literally describe a specific person, place or thing. For example, a [[StatisticalVariable]] Median_Height_Person_Female representing the median height of women, could be written as follows: the population type is [[Person]]; the measuredProperty [[height]]; the [[statType]] [[median]]; the [[gender]] [[Female]]. It is important to note that there are many kinds of scientific quantitative observation which are not fully, perfectly or unambiguously described following this pattern, or with solely Schema.org terminology. The approach taken here is designed to allow partial, incremental or minimal description of [[StatisticalVariable]]s, and the use of detailed sets of entity and property IDs from external repositories. The [[measurementMethod]], [[unitCode]] and [[unitText]] properties can also be used to clarify the specific nature and notation of an observed measurement.
label: StatisticalVariable

comments: Lists or enumerations dealing with status types.
label: StatusEnumeration

comments: A value indicating a steering position.
label: SteeringPositionValue

comments: A retail good store.
label: Store

comments: Structured values are used when the value of a property has a more complex structure than simply being a textual value or a reference to another thing.
label: StructuredValue

comments:
The act of forming a personal connection with someone/something (object) unidirectionally/asymmetrically to get updates pushed to.

Related actions:

* [[FollowAction]]: Unlike FollowAction, SubscribeAction implies that the subscriber acts as a passive agent being constantly/actively pushed for updates.
* [[RegisterAction]]: Unlike RegisterAction, SubscribeAction implies that the agent is interested in continuing receiving updates from the object.
* [[JoinAction]]: Unlike JoinAction, SubscribeAction implies that the agent is interested in continuing receiving updates from the object.
label: SubscribeAction

comments: Any matter of defined composition that has discrete existence, whose origin may be biological, mineral or chemical.
label: Substance

comments: A subway station.
label: SubwayStation

comments:
A suite in a hotel or other public accommodation, denotes a class of luxury accommodations, the key feature of which is multiple rooms (source: Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia, see http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Suite_(hotel)).

See also the dedicated document on the use of schema.org for marking up hotels and other forms of accommodations.
label: Suite

comments: Anatomical features that can be observed by sight (without dissection), including the form and proportions of the human body as well as surface landmarks that correspond to deeper subcutaneous structures. Superficial anatomy plays an important role in sports medicine, phlebotomy, and other medical specialties as underlying anatomical structures can be identified through surface palpation. For example, during back surgery, superficial anatomy can be used to palpate and count vertebrae to find the site of incision. Or in phlebotomy, superficial anatomy can be used to locate an underlying vein; for example, the median cubital vein can be located by palpating the borders of the cubital fossa (such as the epicondyles of the humerus) and then looking for the superficial signs of the vein, such as size, prominence, ability to refill after depression, and feel of surrounding tissue support. As another example, in a subluxation (dislocation) of the glenohumeral joint, the bony structure becomes pronounced with the deltoid muscle failing to cover the glenohumeral joint allowing the edges of the scapula to be superficially visible. Here, the superficial anatomy is the visible edges of the scapula, implying the underlying dislocation of the joint (the related anatomical structure).
label: SuperficialAnatomy

comments: A medical procedure involving an incision with instruments; performed for diagnose, or therapeutic purposes.
label: SurgicalProcedure

comments: The act of momentarily pausing a device or application (e.g. pause music playback or pause a timer).
label: SuspendAction

comments: A syllabus that describes the material covered in a course, often with several such sections per [[Course]] so that a distinct [[timeRequired]] can be provided for that section of the [[Course]].
label: Syllabus

comments: A synagogue.
label: Synagogue

comments: A short TV program or a segment/part of a TV program.
label: TVClip

comments: A TV episode which can be part of a series or season.
label: TVEpisode

comments: Season dedicated to TV broadcast and associated online delivery.
label: TVSeason

comments: CreativeWorkSeries dedicated to TV broadcast and associated online delivery.
label: TVSeries

comments: A table on a Web page.
label: Table

comments:
The act of gaining ownership of an object from an origin. Reciprocal of GiveAction.

Related actions:

* [[GiveAction]]: The reciprocal of TakeAction.
* [[ReceiveAction]]: Unlike ReceiveAction, TakeAction implies that ownership has been transferred.
label: TakeAction

comments: A tattoo parlor.
label: TattooParlor

comments: A taxi.
label: Taxi

comments: A reservation for a taxi.\n\nNote: This type is for information about actual reservations, e.g. in confirmation emails or HTML pages with individual confirmations of reservations. For offers of tickets, use [[Offer]].
label: TaxiReservation

comments: A service for a vehicle for hire with a driver for local travel. Fares are usually calculated based on distance traveled.
label: TaxiService

comments: A taxi stand.
label: TaxiStand

comments: A set of organisms asserted to represent a natural cohesive biological unit.
label: Taxon

comments: A technical article - Example: How-to (task) topics, step-by-step, procedural troubleshooting, specifications, etc.
label: TechArticle

comments: A unique instance of a television BroadcastService on a CableOrSatelliteService lineup.
label: TelevisionChannel

comments: A television station.
label: TelevisionStation

comments: A tennis complex.
label: TennisComplex

comments: Data type: Text.
label: Text

comments: A file composed primarily of text.
label: TextDigitalDocument

comments: A text file. The text can be unformatted or contain markup, html, etc.
label: TextObject

comments: Event type: Theater performance.
label: TheaterEvent

comments: A theater group or company, for example, the Royal Shakespeare Company or Druid Theatre.
label: TheaterGroup

comments: A medical procedure intended primarily for therapeutic purposes, aimed at improving a health condition.
label: TherapeuticProcedure

comments: A thesis or dissertation document submitted in support of candidature for an academic degree or professional qualification.
label: Thesis

comments: The most generic type of item.
label: Thing

comments: Used to describe a ticket to an event, a flight, a bus ride, etc.
label: Ticket

comments: The act of reaching a draw in a competitive activity.
label: TieAction

comments: An enumeration of possible benefits as part of a loyalty (members) program.
label: TierBenefitEnumeration

comments: A point in time recurring on multiple days in the form hh:mm:ss[Z|(+|-)hh:mm] (see [XML schema for details](http://www.w3.org/TR/xmlschema-2/#time)).
label: Time

comments: The act of giving money voluntarily to a beneficiary in recognition of services rendered.
label: TipAction

comments: A tire shop.
label: TireShop

comments: A tourist attraction.  In principle any Thing can be a [[TouristAttraction]], from a [[Mountain]] and [[LandmarksOrHistoricalBuildings]] to a [[LocalBusiness]].  This Type can be used on its own to describe a general [[TouristAttraction]], or be used as an [[additionalType]] to add tourist attraction properties to any other type.  (See examples below)
label: TouristAttraction

comments:
A tourist destination. In principle any [[Place]] can be a [[TouristDestination]] from a [[City]], Region or [[Country]] to an [[AmusementPark]] or [[Hotel]]. This Type can be used on its own to describe a general [[TouristDestination]], or be used as an [[additionalType]] to add tourist relevant properties to any other [[Place]].  A [[TouristDestination]] is defined as a [[Place]] that contains, or is colocated with, one or more [[TouristAttraction]]s, often linked by a similar theme or interest to a particular [[touristType]]. The [UNWTO](http://www2.unwto.org/) defines Destination (main destination of a tourism trip) as the place visited that is central to the decision to take the trip.
  (See examples below.)
label: TouristDestination

comments: A tourist information center.
label: TouristInformationCenter

comments:
A tourist trip. A created itinerary of visits to one or more places of interest ([[TouristAttraction]]/[[TouristDestination]]) often linked by a similar theme, geographic area, or interest to a particular [[touristType]]. The [UNWTO](http://www2.unwto.org/) defines tourism trip as the Trip taken by visitors.
  (See examples below.)
label: TouristTrip

comments: A toy store.
label: ToyStore

comments:
An agent tracks an object for updates.

Related actions:

* [[FollowAction]]: Unlike FollowAction, TrackAction refers to the interest on the location of innanimates objects.
* [[SubscribeAction]]: Unlike SubscribeAction, TrackAction refers to  the interest on the location of innanimate objects.
label: TrackAction

comments: The act of participating in an exchange of goods and services for monetary compensation. An agent trades an object, product or service with a participant in exchange for a one time or periodic payment.
label: TradeAction

comments: A reservation for train travel.\n\nNote: This type is for information about actual reservations, e.g. in confirmation emails or HTML pages with individual confirmations of reservations. For offers of tickets, use [[Offer]].
label: TrainReservation

comments: A train station.
label: TrainStation

comments: A trip on a commercial train line.
label: TrainTrip

comments: The act of transferring/moving (abstract or concrete) animate or inanimate objects from one place to another.
label: TransferAction

comments: The act of traveling from a fromLocation to a destination by a specified mode of transport, optionally with participants.
label: TravelAction

comments: A travel agency.
label: TravelAgency

comments: An indication for treating an underlying condition, symptom, etc.
label: TreatmentIndication

comments: A trip or journey. An itinerary of visits to one or more places.
label: Trip

comments: A structured value indicating the quantity, unit of measurement, and business function of goods included in a bundle offer.
label: TypeAndQuantityNode

comments: UKNonprofitType: Non-profit organization type originating from the United Kingdom.
label: UKNonprofitType

comments: Data type: URL.
label: URL

comments: USNonprofitType: Non-profit organization type originating from the United States.
label: USNonprofitType

comments:
The act of un-registering from a service.

Related actions:

* [[RegisterAction]]: antonym of UnRegisterAction.
* [[LeaveAction]]: Unlike LeaveAction, UnRegisterAction implies that you are unregistering from a service you were previously registered, rather than leaving a team/group of people.
label: UnRegisterAction

comments: The price asked for a given offer by the respective organization or person.
label: UnitPriceSpecification

comments: The act of managing by changing/editing the state of the object.
label: UpdateAction

comments: The act of applying an object to its intended purpose.
label: UseAction

comments: UserInteraction and its subtypes is an old way of talking about users interacting with pages. It is generally better to use [[Action]]-based vocabulary, alongside types such as [[Comment]].
label: UserBlocks

comments: UserInteraction and its subtypes is an old way of talking about users interacting with pages. It is generally better to use [[Action]]-based vocabulary, alongside types such as [[Comment]].
label: UserCheckins

comments: UserInteraction and its subtypes is an old way of talking about users interacting with pages. It is generally better to use [[Action]]-based vocabulary, alongside types such as [[Comment]].
label: UserComments

comments: UserInteraction and its subtypes is an old way of talking about users interacting with pages. It is generally better to use [[Action]]-based vocabulary, alongside types such as [[Comment]].
label: UserDownloads

comments: UserInteraction and its subtypes is an old way of talking about users interacting with pages. It is generally better to use [[Action]]-based vocabulary, alongside types such as [[Comment]].
label: UserInteraction

comments: UserInteraction and its subtypes is an old way of talking about users interacting with pages. It is generally better to use [[Action]]-based vocabulary, alongside types such as [[Comment]].
label: UserLikes

comments: UserInteraction and its subtypes is an old way of talking about users interacting with pages. It is generally better to use [[Action]]-based vocabulary, alongside types such as [[Comment]].
label: UserPageVisits

comments: UserInteraction and its subtypes is an old way of talking about users interacting with pages. It is generally better to use [[Action]]-based vocabulary, alongside types such as [[Comment]].
label: UserPlays

comments: UserInteraction and its subtypes is an old way of talking about users interacting with pages. It is generally better to use [[Action]]-based vocabulary, alongside types such as [[Comment]].
label: UserPlusOnes

comments: A review created by an end-user (e.g. consumer, purchaser, attendee etc.), in contrast with [[CriticReview]].
label: UserReview

comments: UserInteraction and its subtypes is an old way of talking about users interacting with pages. It is generally better to use [[Action]]-based vocabulary, alongside types such as [[Comment]].
label: UserTweets

comments: A kind of lodging business that focuses on renting single properties for limited time.
label: VacationRental

comments: A vehicle is a device that is designed or used to transport people or cargo over land, water, air, or through space.
label: Vehicle

comments: A type of blood vessel that specifically carries blood to the heart.
label: Vein

comments: A component of the human body circulatory system comprised of an intricate network of hollow tubes that transport blood throughout the entire body.
label: Vessel

comments: A vet's office.
label: VeterinaryCare

comments: Web page type: Video gallery page.
label: VideoGallery

comments: A video game is an electronic game that involves human interaction with a user interface to generate visual feedback on a video device.
label: VideoGame

comments: A short segment/part of a video game.
label: VideoGameClip

comments: A video game series.
label: VideoGameSeries

comments: A video file.
label: VideoObject

comments: A specific and exact (byte-for-byte) version of a [[VideoObject]]. Two byte-for-byte identical files, for the purposes of this type, considered identical. If they have different embedded metadata the files will differ. Different external facts about the files, e.g. creator or dateCreated that aren't represented in their actual content, do not affect this notion of identity.
label: VideoObjectSnapshot

comments: The act of consuming static visual content.
label: ViewAction

comments: An online or virtual location for attending events. For example, one may attend an online seminar or educational event. While a virtual location may be used as the location of an event, virtual locations should not be confused with physical locations in the real world.
label: VirtualLocation

comments: Event type: Visual arts event.
label: VisualArtsEvent

comments: A work of art that is primarily visual in character.
label: VisualArtwork

comments: Vital signs are measures of various physiological functions in order to assess the most basic body functions.
label: VitalSign

comments: A volcano, like Fujisan.
label: Volcano

comments: The act of expressing a preference from a fixed/finite/structured set of choices/options.
label: VoteAction

comments: An advertising section of the page.
label: WPAdBlock

comments: The footer section of the page.
label: WPFooter

comments: The header section of the page.
label: WPHeader

comments: A sidebar section of the page.
label: WPSideBar

comments: The act of expressing a desire about the object. An agent wants an object.
label: WantAction

comments: A structured value representing the duration and scope of services that will be provided to a customer free of charge in case of a defect or malfunction of a product.
label: WarrantyPromise

comments:
A range of services that will be provided to a customer free of charge in case of a defect or malfunction of a product.

Commonly used values:

* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#Labor-BringIn
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#PartsAndLabor-BringIn
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#PartsAndLabor-PickUp
label: WarrantyScope

comments: The act of consuming dynamic/moving visual content.
label: WatchAction

comments: A waterfall, like Niagara.
label: Waterfall

comments: The act of dressing oneself in clothing.
label: WearAction

comments: Enumerates common types of measurement for wearables products.
label: WearableMeasurementTypeEnumeration

comments: Enumerates common size groups (also known as "size types") for wearable products.
label: WearableSizeGroupEnumeration

comments: Enumerates common size systems specific for wearable products.
label: WearableSizeSystemEnumeration

comments: An application programming interface accessible over Web/Internet technologies.
label: WebAPI

comments: Web applications.
label: WebApplication

comments: WebContent is a type representing all [[WebPage]], [[WebSite]] and [[WebPageElement]] content. It is sometimes the case that detailed distinctions between Web pages, sites and their parts are not always important or obvious. The  [[WebContent]] type makes it easier to describe Web-addressable content without requiring such distinctions to always be stated. (The intent is that the existing types [[WebPage]], [[WebSite]] and [[WebPageElement]] will eventually be declared as subtypes of [[WebContent]].)
label: WebContent

comments: A web page. Every web page is implicitly assumed to be declared to be of type WebPage, so the various properties about that webpage, such as breadcrumb may be used. We recommend explicit declaration if these properties are specified, but if they are found outside of an itemscope, they will be assumed to be about the page.
label: WebPage

comments: A web page element, like a table or an image.
label: WebPageElement

comments: A WebSite is a set of related web pages and other items typically served from a single web domain and accessible via URLs.
label: WebSite

comments: A wholesale store.
label: WholesaleStore

comments: The act of achieving victory in a competitive activity.
label: WinAction

comments: A winery.
label: Winery

comments: A program with both an educational and employment component. Typically based at a workplace and structured around work-based learning, with the aim of instilling competencies related to an occupation. WorkBasedProgram is used to distinguish programs such as apprenticeships from school, college or other classroom based educational programs.
label: WorkBasedProgram

comments: A Workers Union (also known as a Labor Union, Labour Union, or Trade Union) is an organization that promotes the interests of its worker members by collectively bargaining with management, organizing, and political lobbying.
label: WorkersUnion

comments: The act of authoring written creative content.
label: WriteAction

comments: Text representing an XPath (typically but not necessarily version 1.0).
label: XPathType

comments: A zoo.
label: Zoo

comments: The subject matter of an object.
label: about

comments: Indicates whether the book is an abridged edition.
label: abridged

comments: An abstract is a short description that summarizes a [[CreativeWork]].
label: abstract

comments:
The time needed to accelerate the vehicle from a given start velocity to a given target velocity.

Typical unit code(s): SEC for seconds

* Note: There are unfortunately no standard unit codes for seconds/0..100 km/h or seconds/0..60 mph. Simply use "SEC" for seconds and indicate the velocities in the [[name]] of the [[QuantitativeValue]], or use [[valueReference]] with a [[QuantitativeValue]] of 0..60 mph or 0..100 km/h to specify the reference speeds.
label: accelerationTime

comments: The answer(s) that has been accepted as best, typically on a Question/Answer site. Sites vary in their selection mechanisms, e.g. drawing on community opinion and/or the view of the Question author.
label: acceptedAnswer

comments: The offer(s) -- e.g., product, quantity and price combinations -- included in the order.
label: acceptedOffer

comments: The payment method(s) that are accepted in general by an organization, or for some specific demand or offer.
label: acceptedPaymentMethod

comments: Indicates whether a FoodEstablishment accepts reservations. Values can be Boolean, an URL at which reservations can be made or (for backwards compatibility) the strings ```Yes``` or ```No```.
label: acceptsReservations

comments: Password, PIN, or access code needed for delivery (e.g. from a locker).
label: accessCode

comments: The human sensory perceptual system or cognitive faculty through which a person may process or perceive the intellectual content of a resource, not including any adaptations of the content (e.g., text alternatives for images). Values should be drawn from the [approved vocabulary](https://www.w3.org/2021/a11y-discov-vocab/latest/#accessMode-vocabulary).
label: accessMode

comments: A list of single or combined access modes that are sufficient to understand all the intellectual content of a resource, including any adaptations. Values should be drawn from the [approved vocabulary](https://www.w3.org/2021/a11y-discov-vocab/latest/#accessModeSufficient-vocabulary).
label: accessModeSufficient

comments: Indicates that the resource is compatible with the referenced accessibility API. Values should be drawn from the [approved vocabulary](https://www.w3.org/2021/a11y-discov-vocab/latest/#accessibilityAPI-vocabulary).
label: accessibilityAPI

comments: Identifies input methods that are sufficient to fully control the described resource. Values should be drawn from the [approved vocabulary](https://www.w3.org/2021/a11y-discov-vocab/latest/#accessibilityControl-vocabulary).
label: accessibilityControl

comments: Content features of the resource, such as accessible media, alternatives and supported enhancements for accessibility. Values should be drawn from the [approved vocabulary](https://www.w3.org/2021/a11y-discov-vocab/latest/#accessibilityFeature-vocabulary).
label: accessibilityFeature

comments: A characteristic of the described resource that is physiologically dangerous to some users. Related to WCAG 2.0 guideline 2.3. Values should be drawn from the [approved vocabulary](https://www.w3.org/2021/a11y-discov-vocab/latest/#accessibilityHazard-vocabulary).
label: accessibilityHazard

comments: A human-readable summary of specific accessibility features or deficiencies, consistent with the other accessibility metadata but expressing subtleties such as "short descriptions are present but long descriptions will be needed for non-visual users" or "short descriptions are present and no long descriptions are needed".
label: accessibilitySummary

comments: Category of an [[Accommodation]], following real estate conventions, e.g. RESO (see [PropertySubType](https://ddwiki.reso.org/display/DDW17/PropertySubType+Field), and [PropertyType](https://ddwiki.reso.org/display/DDW17/PropertyType+Field) fields  for suggested values).
label: accommodationCategory

comments: A floorplan of some [[Accommodation]].
label: accommodationFloorPlan

comments: The identifier for the account the payment will be applied to.
label: accountId

comments: A minimum amount that has to be paid in every month.
label: accountMinimumInflow

comments: An overdraft is an extension of credit from a lending institution when an account reaches zero. An overdraft allows the individual to continue withdrawing money even if the account has no funds in it. Basically the bank allows people to borrow a set amount of money.
label: accountOverdraftLimit

comments: Specifies the Person that is legally accountable for the CreativeWork.
label: accountablePerson

comments: Indicates a page documenting how licenses can be purchased or otherwise acquired, for the current item.
label: acquireLicensePage

comments: The organization or person from which the product was acquired.
label: acquiredFrom

comments: The ACRISS Car Classification Code is a code used by many car rental companies, for classifying vehicles. ACRISS stands for Association of Car Rental Industry Systems and Standards.
label: acrissCode

comments: A set of requirements that must be fulfilled in order to perform an Action. If more than one value is specified, fulfilling one set of requirements will allow the Action to be performed.
label: actionAccessibilityRequirement

comments: An application that can complete the request.
label: actionApplication

comments: A sub property of object. The options subject to this action.
label: actionOption

comments: The high level platform(s) where the Action can be performed for the given URL. To specify a specific application or operating system instance, use actionApplication.
label: actionPlatform

comments: Description of the process by which the action was performed.
label: actionProcess

comments: Indicates the current disposition of the Action.
label: actionStatus

comments: For a [[NewsMediaOrganization]] or other news-related [[Organization]], a statement about public engagement activities (for news media, the newsroom’s), including involving the public - digitally or otherwise -- in coverage decisions, reporting and activities after publication.
label: actionableFeedbackPolicy

comments: An active ingredient, typically chemical compounds and/or biologic substances.
label: activeIngredient

comments: Length of time to engage in the activity.
label: activityDuration

comments: How often one should engage in the activity.
label: activityFrequency

comments: An actor (individual or a group), e.g. in TV, radio, movie, video games etc., or in an event. Actors can be associated with individual items or with a series, episode, clip.
label: actor

comments: An actor, e.g. in TV, radio, movie, video games etc. Actors can be associated with individual items or with a series, episode, clip.
label: actors

comments: An additional offer that can only be obtained in combination with the first base offer (e.g. supplements and extensions that are available for a surcharge).
label: addOn

comments: An additional name for a Person, can be used for a middle name.
label: additionalName

comments: If responding yes, the number of guests who will attend in addition to the invitee.
label: additionalNumberOfGuests

comments: A property-value pair representing an additional characteristic of the entity, e.g. a product feature or another characteristic for which there is no matching property in schema.org.\n\nNote: Publishers should be aware that applications designed to use specific schema.org properties (e.g. https://schema.org/width, https://schema.org/color, https://schema.org/gtin13, ...) will typically expect such data to be provided using those properties, rather than using the generic property/value mechanism.
label: additionalProperty

comments: An additional type for the item, typically used for adding more specific types from external vocabularies in microdata syntax. This is a relationship between something and a class that the thing is in. Typically the value is a URI-identified RDF class, and in this case corresponds to the use of rdf:type in RDF. Text values can be used sparingly, for cases where useful information can be added without their being an appropriate schema to reference. In the case of text values, the class label should follow the schema.org style guide.
label: additionalType

comments: Any additional component of the exercise prescription that may need to be articulated to the patient. This may include the order of exercises, the number of repetitions of movement, quantitative distance, progressions over time, etc.
label: additionalVariable

comments: Physical address of the item.
label: address

comments: The country. Recommended to be in 2-letter [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1) format, for example "US". For backward compatibility, a 3-letter [ISO 3166-1 alpha-3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-3) country code such as "SGP" or a full country name such as "Singapore" can also be used.
label: addressCountry

comments: The locality in which the street address is, and which is in the region. For example, Mountain View.
label: addressLocality

comments: The region in which the locality is, and which is in the country. For example, California or another appropriate first-level [Administrative division](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_administrative_divisions_by_country) such as the Province in Italy or Region in Germany.
label: addressRegion

comments: A route by which this drug may be administered, e.g. 'oral'.
label: administrationRoute

comments: The amount of time that is required between accepting the offer and the actual usage of the resource or service.
label: advanceBookingRequirement

comments: A possible complication and/or side effect of this therapy. If it is known that an adverse outcome is serious (resulting in death, disability, or permanent damage; requiring hospitalization; or otherwise life-threatening or requiring immediate medical attention), tag it as a seriousAdverseOutcome instead.
label: adverseOutcome

comments: Drugs that affect the test's results.
label: affectedBy

comments: An organization that this person is affiliated with. For example, a school/university, a club, or a team.
label: affiliation

comments: A media object representing the circumstances after performing this direction.
label: afterMedia

comments: The direct performer or driver of the action (animate or inanimate). E.g. *John* wrote a book.
label: agent

comments: The number of completed interactions for this entity, in a particular role (the 'agent'), in a particular action (indicated in the statistic), and in a particular context (i.e. interactionService).
label: agentInteractionStatistic

comments: Indicates a prototype of the elements in the list that is used to hold aggregate information (ratings, offers, etc.).
label: aggregateElement

comments: The overall rating, based on a collection of reviews or ratings, of the item.
label: aggregateRating

comments: The kind of aircraft (e.g., "Boeing 747").
label: aircraft

comments: A music album.
label: album

comments: Classification of the album by its type of content: soundtrack, live album, studio album, etc.
label: albumProductionType

comments: A release of this album.
label: albumRelease

comments: The kind of release which this album is: single, EP or album.
label: albumReleaseType

comments: A collection of music albums.
label: albums

comments: Any precaution, guidance, contraindication, etc. related to consumption of alcohol while taking this drug.
label: alcoholWarning

comments: The algorithm or rules to follow to compute the score.
label: algorithm

comments: A category of alignment between the learning resource and the framework node. Recommended values include: 'requires', 'textComplexity', 'readingLevel', and 'educationalSubject'.
label: alignmentType

comments: An alias for the item.
label: alternateName

comments: A secondary title of the CreativeWork.
label: alternativeHeadline

comments: Another gene which is a variation of this one.
label: alternativeOf

comments: Alumni of an organization.
label: alumni

comments: An organization that the person is an alumni of.
label: alumniOf

comments: An amenity feature (e.g. a characteristic or service) of the Accommodation. This generic property does not make a statement about whether the feature is included in an offer for the main accommodation or available at extra costs.
label: amenityFeature

comments: The amount of money.
label: amount

comments: The quantity of the goods included in the offer.
label: amountOfThisGood

comments: Indicates a specific [[CivicStructure]] or [[LocalBusiness]] associated with the SpecialAnnouncement. For example, a specific testing facility or business with special opening hours. For a larger geographic region like a quarantine of an entire region, use [[spatialCoverage]].
label: announcementLocation

comments: The annual rate that is charged for borrowing (or made by investing), expressed as a single percentage number that represents the actual yearly cost of funds over the term of a loan. This includes any fees or additional costs associated with the transaction.
label: annualPercentageRate

comments: The number of answers this question has received.
label: answerCount

comments: A step-by-step or full explanation about Answer. Can outline how this Answer was achieved or contain more broad clarification or statement about it.
label: answerExplanation

comments: The muscle whose action counteracts the specified muscle.
label: antagonist

comments: Indicates an occurrence of a [[Claim]] in some [[CreativeWork]].
label: appearance

comments: A country where a particular merchant return policy applies to, for example the two-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code.
label: applicableCountry

comments: The location in which the status applies.
label: applicableLocation

comments: The location(s) applicants can apply from. This is usually used for telecommuting jobs where the applicant does not need to be in a physical office. Note: This should not be used for citizenship or work visa requirements.
label: applicantLocationRequirements

comments: An application that can complete the request.
label: application

comments: Type of software application, e.g. 'Game, Multimedia'.
label: applicationCategory

comments: Contact details for further information relevant to this job posting.
label: applicationContact

comments: The date on which the program stops collecting applications for the next enrollment cycle. Flexible application deadlines (for example, a program with rolling admissions) can be described in a textual string, rather than as a DateTime.
label: applicationDeadline

comments: The date at which the program begins collecting applications for the next enrollment cycle.
label: applicationStartDate

comments: Subcategory of the application, e.g. 'Arcade Game'.
label: applicationSubCategory

comments: The name of the application suite to which the application belongs (e.g. Excel belongs to Office).
label: applicationSuite

comments: The delivery method(s) to which the delivery charge or payment charge specification applies.
label: appliesToDeliveryMethod

comments: The payment method(s) to which the payment charge specification applies.
label: appliesToPaymentMethod

comments: Collection, [fonds](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fonds), or item held, kept or maintained by an [[ArchiveOrganization]].
label: archiveHeld

comments: Indicates a page or other link involved in archival of a [[CreativeWork]]. In the case of [[MediaReview]], the items in a [[MediaReviewItem]] may often become inaccessible, but be archived by archival, journalistic, activist, or law enforcement organizations. In such cases, the referenced page may not directly publish the content.
label: archivedAt

comments: The area within which users can expect to reach the broadcast service.
label: area

comments: The geographic area where a service or offered item is provided.
label: areaServed

comments: The airport where the flight terminates.
label: arrivalAirport

comments: The terminal or port from which the boat arrives.
label: arrivalBoatTerminal

comments: The stop or station from which the bus arrives.
label: arrivalBusStop

comments: Identifier of the flight's arrival gate.
label: arrivalGate

comments: The platform where the train arrives.
label: arrivalPlatform

comments: The station where the train trip ends.
label: arrivalStation

comments: Identifier of the flight's arrival terminal.
label: arrivalTerminal

comments: The expected arrival time.
label: arrivalTime

comments: The number of copies when multiple copies of a piece of artwork are produced - e.g. for a limited edition of 20 prints, 'artEdition' refers to the total number of copies (in this example "20").
label: artEdition

comments: The material used. (E.g. Oil, Watercolour, Acrylic, Linoprint, Marble, Cyanotype, Digital, Lithograph, DryPoint, Intaglio, Pastel, Woodcut, Pencil, Mixed Media, etc.)
label: artMedium

comments: The branches that comprise the arterial structure.
label: arterialBranch

comments: e.g. Painting, Drawing, Sculpture, Print, Photograph, Assemblage, Collage, etc.
label: artform

comments: The actual body of the article.
label: articleBody

comments: Articles may belong to one or more 'sections' in a magazine or newspaper, such as Sports, Lifestyle, etc.
label: articleSection

comments:
The primary artist for a work
    	in a medium other than pencils or digital line art--for example, if the
    	primary artwork is done in watercolors or digital paints.
label: artist

comments: The supporting materials for the artwork, e.g. Canvas, Paper, Wood, Board, etc.
label: artworkSurface

comments:
An Amazon Standard Identification Number (ASIN) is a 10-character alphanumeric unique identifier assigned by Amazon.com and its partners for product identification within the Amazon organization (summary from [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amazon_Standard_Identification_Number)'s article).

Note also that this is a definition for how to include ASINs in Schema.org data, and not a definition of ASINs in general - see documentation from Amazon for authoritative details.
ASINs are most commonly encoded as text strings, but the [asin] property supports URL/URI as potential values too.
label: asin

comments: An aspect of medical practice that is considered on the page, such as 'diagnosis', 'treatment', 'causes', 'prognosis', 'etiology', 'epidemiology', etc.
label: aspect

comments: Library file name, e.g., mscorlib.dll, system.web.dll.
label: assembly

comments: Associated product/technology version. E.g., .NET Framework 4.5.
label: assemblyVersion

comments: The item being described is intended to assess the competency or learning outcome defined by the referenced term.
label: assesses

comments: The anatomy of the underlying organ system or structures associated with this entity.
label: associatedAnatomy

comments: A NewsArticle associated with the Media Object.
label: associatedArticle

comments: An associated [[ClaimReview]], related by specific common content, topic or claim. The expectation is that this property would be most typically used in cases where a single activity is conducting both claim reviews and media reviews, in which case [[relatedMediaReview]] would commonly be used on a [[ClaimReview]], while [[associatedClaimReview]] would be used on [[MediaReview]].
label: associatedClaimReview

comments: Disease associated to this BioChemEntity. Such disease can be a MedicalCondition or a URL. If you want to add an evidence supporting the association, please use PropertyValue.
label: associatedDisease

comments: A media object that encodes this CreativeWork. This property is a synonym for encoding.
label: associatedMedia

comments: An associated [[MediaReview]], related by specific common content, topic or claim. The expectation is that this property would be most typically used in cases where a single activity is conducting both claim reviews and media reviews, in which case [[relatedMediaReview]] would commonly be used on a [[ClaimReview]], while [[associatedClaimReview]] would be used on [[MediaReview]].
label: associatedMediaReview

comments: If applicable, a description of the pathophysiology associated with the anatomical system, including potential abnormal changes in the mechanical, physical, and biochemical functions of the system.
label: associatedPathophysiology

comments: An associated [[Review]].
label: associatedReview

comments: A person that acts as performing member of a sports team; a player as opposed to a coach.
label: athlete

comments: A person or organization attending the event.
label: attendee

comments: A person attending the event.
label: attendees

comments: An intended audience, i.e. a group for whom something was created.
label: audience

comments: The target group associated with a given audience (e.g. veterans, car owners, musicians, etc.).
label: audienceType

comments: An embedded audio object.
label: audio

comments: Date when a certification was last audited. See also  [gs1:certificationAuditDate](https://www.gs1.org/voc/certificationAuditDate).
label: auditDate

comments: The Organization responsible for authenticating the user's subscription. For example, many media apps require a cable/satellite provider to authenticate your subscription before playing media.
label: authenticator

comments: The author of this content or rating. Please note that author is special in that HTML 5 provides a special mechanism for indicating authorship via the rel tag. That is equivalent to this and may be used interchangeably.
label: author

comments: The availability of this item—for example In stock, Out of stock, Pre-order, etc.
label: availability

comments: The end of the availability of the product or service included in the offer.
label: availabilityEnds

comments: The beginning of the availability of the product or service included in the offer.
label: availabilityStarts

comments: The place(s) from which the offer can be obtained (e.g. store locations).
label: availableAtOrFrom

comments: A means of accessing the service (e.g. a phone bank, a web site, a location, etc.).
label: availableChannel

comments: The delivery method(s) available for this offer.
label: availableDeliveryMethod

comments: When the item is available for pickup from the store, locker, etc.
label: availableFrom

comments: The location in which the strength is available.
label: availableIn

comments: A language someone may use with or at the item, service or place. Please use one of the language codes from the [IETF BCP 47 standard](http://tools.ietf.org/html/bcp47). See also [[inLanguage]].
label: availableLanguage

comments: Device required to run the application. Used in cases where a specific make/model is required to run the application.
label: availableOnDevice

comments: A medical service available from this provider.
label: availableService

comments: An available dosage strength for the drug.
label: availableStrength

comments: A diagnostic test or procedure offered by this lab.
label: availableTest

comments: After this date, the item will no longer be available for pickup.
label: availableThrough

comments: An award won by or for this item.
label: award

comments: Awards won by or for this item.
label: awards

comments: The away team in a sports event.
label: awayTeam

comments: For an [[Article]], typically a [[NewsArticle]], the backstory property provides a textual summary giving a brief explanation of why and how an article was created. In a journalistic setting this could include information about reporting process, methods, interviews, data sources, etc.
label: backstory

comments: The type of a bank account.
label: bankAccountType

comments: The base salary of the job or of an employee in an EmployeeRole.
label: baseSalary

comments: A sub property of recipient. The recipient blind copied on a message.
label: bccRecipient

comments:
The type of bed or beds included in the accommodation. For the single case of just one bed of a certain type, you use bed directly with a text.
      If you want to indicate the quantity of a certain kind of bed, use an instance of BedDetails. For more detailed information, use the amenityFeature property.
label: bed

comments: A media object representing the circumstances before performing this direction.
label: beforeMedia

comments: A bank or bank’s branch, financial institution or international financial institution operating the beneficiary’s bank account or releasing funds for the beneficiary.
label: beneficiaryBank

comments: Description of benefits associated with the job.
label: benefits

comments: The URL that goes directly to the summary of benefits and coverage for the specific standard plan or plan variation.
label: benefitsSummaryUrl

comments: The highest value allowed in this rating system.
label: bestRating

comments: The billing address for the order.
label: billingAddress

comments: Specifies for how long this price (or price component) will be billed. Can be used, for example, to model the contractual duration of a subscription or payment plan. Type can be either a Duration or a Number (in which case the unit of measurement, for example month, is specified by the unitCode property).
label: billingDuration

comments: This property specifies the minimal quantity and rounding increment that will be the basis for the billing. The unit of measurement is specified by the unitCode property.
label: billingIncrement

comments: The time interval used to compute the invoice.
label: billingPeriod

comments: Specifies after how much time this price (or price component) becomes valid and billing starts. Can be used, for example, to model a price increase after the first year of a subscription. The unit of measurement is specified by the unitCode property.
label: billingStart

comments: A BioChemEntity that is known to interact with this item.
label: bioChemInteraction

comments: A similar BioChemEntity, e.g., obtained by fingerprint similarity algorithms.
label: bioChemSimilarity

comments: A role played by the BioChemEntity within a biological context.
label: biologicalRole

comments: The biomechanical properties of the bone.
label: biomechnicalClass

comments: Date of birth.
label: birthDate

comments: The place where the person was born.
label: birthPlace

comments: The bitrate of the media object.
label: bitrate

comments: A posting that is part of this blog.
label: blogPost

comments: Indicates a post that is part of a [[Blog]]. Note that historically, what we term a "Blog" was once known as a "weblog", and that what we term a "BlogPosting" is now often colloquially referred to as a "blog".
label: blogPosts

comments: The blood vessel that carries blood from the heart to the muscle.
label: bloodSupply

comments: The airline-specific indicator of boarding order / preference.
label: boardingGroup

comments: The type of boarding policy used by the airline (e.g. zone-based or group-based).
label: boardingPolicy

comments: Location in the body of the anatomical structure.
label: bodyLocation

comments: Indicates the design and body style of the vehicle (e.g. station wagon, hatchback, etc.).
label: bodyType

comments: The edition of the book.
label: bookEdition

comments: The format of the book.
label: bookFormat

comments: 'bookingAgent' is an out-dated term indicating a 'broker' that serves as a booking agent.
label: bookingAgent

comments: The date and time the reservation was booked.
label: bookingTime

comments: A sub property of participant. The person that borrows the object being lent.
label: borrower

comments: A box is the area enclosed by the rectangle formed by two points. The first point is the lower corner, the second point is the upper corner. A box is expressed as two points separated by a space character.
label: box

comments: The branches that delineate from the nerve bundle. Not to be confused with [[branchOf]].
label: branch

comments: A short textual code (also called "store code") that uniquely identifies a place of business. The code is typically assigned by the parentOrganization and used in structured URLs.\n\nFor example, in the URL http://www.starbucks.co.uk/store-locator/etc/detail/3047 the code "3047" is a branchCode for a particular branch.
label: branchCode

comments: The larger organization that this local business is a branch of, if any. Not to be confused with (anatomical) [[branch]].
label: branchOf

comments: The brand(s) associated with a product or service, or the brand(s) maintained by an organization or business person.
label: brand

comments: A set of links that can help a user understand and navigate a website hierarchy.
label: breadcrumb

comments: Any precaution, guidance, contraindication, etc. related to this drug's use by breastfeeding mothers.
label: breastfeedingWarning

comments: The media network(s) whose content is broadcast on this station.
label: broadcastAffiliateOf

comments: The unique address by which the BroadcastService can be identified in a provider lineup. In US, this is typically a number.
label: broadcastChannelId

comments: The name displayed in the channel guide. For many US affiliates, it is the network name.
label: broadcastDisplayName

comments: The frequency used for over-the-air broadcasts. Numeric values or simple ranges, e.g. 87-99. In addition a shortcut idiom is supported for frequencies of AM and FM radio channels, e.g. "87 FM".
label: broadcastFrequency

comments: The frequency in MHz for a particular broadcast.
label: broadcastFrequencyValue

comments: The event being broadcast such as a sporting event or awards ceremony.
label: broadcastOfEvent

comments: The type of service required to have access to the channel (e.g. Standard or Premium).
label: broadcastServiceTier

comments: The modulation (e.g. FM, AM, etc) used by a particular broadcast service.
label: broadcastSignalModulation

comments: The subchannel used for the broadcast.
label: broadcastSubChannel

comments: The timezone in [ISO 8601 format](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) for which the service bases its broadcasts.
label: broadcastTimezone

comments: The organization owning or operating the broadcast service.
label: broadcaster

comments: An entity that arranges for an exchange between a buyer and a seller.  In most cases a broker never acquires or releases ownership of a product or service involved in an exchange.  If it is not clear whether an entity is a broker, seller, or buyer, the latter two terms are preferred.
label: broker

comments: Specifies browser requirements in human-readable text. For example, 'requires HTML5 support'.
label: browserRequirements

comments: The name of the bus (e.g. Bolt Express).
label: busName

comments: The unique identifier for the bus.
label: busNumber

comments: Days of the week when the merchant typically operates, indicated via opening hours markup.
label: businessDays

comments: The business function (e.g. sell, lease, repair, dispose) of the offer or component of a bundle (TypeAndQuantityNode). The default is http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#Sell.
label: businessFunction

comments: A sub property of participant. The participant/person/organization that bought the object.
label: buyer

comments: The artist that performed this album or recording.
label: byArtist

comments: Defines the day(s) of the week on which a recurring [[Event]] takes place. May be specified using either [[DayOfWeek]], or alternatively [[Text]] conforming to iCal's syntax for byDay recurrence rules.
label: byDay

comments: Defines the month(s) of the year on which a recurring [[Event]] takes place. Specified as an [[Integer]] between 1-12. January is 1.
label: byMonth

comments: Defines the day(s) of the month on which a recurring [[Event]] takes place. Specified as an [[Integer]] between 1-31.
label: byMonthDay

comments: Defines the week(s) of the month on which a recurring Event takes place. Specified as an Integer between 1-5. For clarity, byMonthWeek is best used in conjunction with byDay to indicate concepts like the first and third Mondays of a month.
label: byMonthWeek

comments: A [callsign](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Call_sign), as used in broadcasting and radio communications to identify people, radio and TV stations, or vehicles.
label: callSign

comments: The number of calories.
label: calories

comments: A sub property of object. The candidate subject of this action.
label: candidate

comments: The caption for this object. For downloadable machine formats (closed caption, subtitles etc.) use MediaObject and indicate the [[encodingFormat]].
label: caption

comments: The number of grams of carbohydrates.
label: carbohydrateContent

comments:
The available volume for cargo or luggage. For automobiles, this is usually the trunk volume.

Typical unit code(s): LTR for liters, FTQ for cubic foot/feet

Note: You can use [[minValue]] and [[maxValue]] to indicate ranges.
label: cargoVolume

comments: 'carrier' is an out-dated term indicating the 'provider' for parcel delivery and flights.
label: carrier

comments: Specifies specific carrier(s) requirements for the application (e.g. an application may only work on a specific carrier network).
label: carrierRequirements

comments: A cardholder benefit that pays the cardholder a small percentage of their net expenditures.
label: cashBack

comments: A data catalog which contains this dataset.
label: catalog

comments: The catalog number for the release.
label: catalogNumber

comments: A category for the item. Greater signs or slashes can be used to informally indicate a category hierarchy.
label: category

comments: The cause of a medical condition.
label: cause

comments: The condition, complication, symptom, sign, etc. caused.
label: causeOf

comments: A sub property of recipient. The recipient copied on a message.
label: ccRecipient

comments: Identifier of a certification instance (as registered with an independent certification body). Typically this identifier can be used to consult and verify the certification instance. See also [gs1:certificationIdentification](https://www.gs1.org/voc/certificationIdentification).
label: certificationIdentification

comments: Rating of a certification instance (as defined by an independent certification body). Typically this rating can be used to rate the level to which the requirements of the certification instance are fulfilled. See also [gs1:certificationValue](https://www.gs1.org/voc/certificationValue).
label: certificationRating

comments: Indicates the current status of a certification: active or inactive. See also  [gs1:certificationStatus](https://www.gs1.org/voc/certificationStatus).
label: certificationStatus

comments: Fictional person connected with a creative work.
label: character

comments: A piece of data that represents a particular aspect of a fictional character (skill, power, character points, advantage, disadvantage).
label: characterAttribute

comments: The name of a character played in some acting or performing role, i.e. in a PerformanceRole.
label: characterName

comments: Cheat codes to the game.
label: cheatCode

comments: The earliest someone may check into a lodging establishment.
label: checkinTime

comments: A URL template (RFC 6570) for a checkout page for an offer. This approach allows merchants to specify a URL for online checkout of the offered product, by interpolating parameters such as the logged in user ID, product ID, quantity, discount code etc. Parameter naming and standardization are not specified here.
label: checkoutPageURLTemplate

comments: The latest someone may check out of a lodging establishment.
label: checkoutTime

comments: The chemical composition describes the identity and relative ratio of the chemical elements that make up the substance.
label: chemicalComposition

comments: A role played by the BioChemEntity within a chemical context.
label: chemicalRole

comments: Maximal age of the child.
label: childMaxAge

comments: Minimal age of the child.
label: childMinAge

comments: Closest child taxa of the taxon in question.
label: childTaxon

comments: A child of the person.
label: children

comments: The number of milligrams of cholesterol.
label: cholesterolContent

comments: A circle is the circular region of a specified radius centered at a specified latitude and longitude. A circle is expressed as a pair followed by a radius in meters.
label: circle

comments: A citation or reference to another creative work, such as another publication, web page, scholarly article, etc.
label: citation

comments: For a [[Claim]] interpreted from [[MediaObject]] content, the [[interpretedAsClaim]] property can be used to indicate a claim contained, implied or refined from the content of a [[MediaObject]].
label: claimInterpreter

comments: A short summary of the specific claims reviewed in a ClaimReview.
label: claimReviewed

comments: Description of the absorption and elimination of drugs, including their concentration (pharmacokinetics, pK) and biological effects (pharmacodynamics, pD).
label: clincalPharmacology

comments: Description of the absorption and elimination of drugs, including their concentration (pharmacokinetics, pK) and biological effects (pharmacodynamics, pD).
label: clinicalPharmacology

comments: Position of the clip within an ordered group of clips.
label: clipNumber

comments: The closing hour of the place or service on the given day(s) of the week.
label: closes

comments: A person that acts in a coaching role for a sports team.
label: coach

comments: A medical code for the entity, taken from a controlled vocabulary or ontology such as ICD-9, DiseasesDB, MeSH, SNOMED-CT, RxNorm, etc.
label: code

comments: Link to the repository where the un-compiled, human readable code and related code is located (SVN, GitHub, CodePlex).
label: codeRepository

comments: What type of code sample: full (compile ready) solution, code snippet, inline code, scripts, template.
label: codeSampleType

comments: A short textual code that uniquely identifies the value.
label: codeValue

comments: The coding system, e.g. 'ICD-10'.
label: codingSystem

comments: A colleague of the person.
label: colleague

comments: A colleague of the person.
label: colleagues

comments: A sub property of object. The collection target of the action.
label: collection

comments: The number of items in the [[Collection]].
